2605.28607.title: Adaptive Multimodal Agents-Based Framework for Automatic Workflow Execution
2605.28607.paper_summary: Modern information systems require autonomous agents capable of navigating complex workflows, yet current methodologies often struggle with the transition from structured metadata parsing to general environmental perception. While the integration of MLLMs has enabled agents to interact directly with GUIs, existing approaches typically treat task sequences as discrete, linear episodes. This fragmentation prevents agents from capturing the underlying transition topology, limiting their effectiveness in novel or non-stationary scenarios. To address this, we propose a novel multimodal multi-agent framework that achieves automatic workflow execution through a distinct two-phase pipeline. First, during an offline discovery phase, the architecture adaptively constructs a topological knowledge base from fragmented execution logs. During inference, agents leverage Adaptive Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) over this fixed, pre-established graph, coupled with a closed-loop collaborative verification protocol to dynamically self-correct and navigate. This graph-based approach facilitates superior task decomposition and adaptive navigation performance. We validate our framework in a real-world context, demonstrating its ability to maintain high reliability and semantic awareness even with limited training data.
2605.28607.paper_summary_zh: 現代資訊系統需要能夠自主導航複雜工作流程的代理，但當前的方法論在從結構化元數據解析過渡到一般環境感知時常常面臨困難。雖然MLLM的整合使得代理能夠直接與GUI互動，但現有的方法通常將任務序列視為離散的線性事件。這種碎片化阻礙了代理捕捉潛在的轉換拓撲，限制了它們在新穎或非穩態場景中的有效性。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一種新穎的多模態多代理框架，通過一個獨特的兩階段管道實現自動工作流程執行。首先，在離線發現階段，該架構自適應地從碎片化的執行日誌中構建拓撲知識庫。在推理過程中，代理利用自適應檢索增強生成（RAG）在這個固定的、預先建立的圖上，並結合閉環協作驗證協議進行動態自我修正和導航。這種基於圖的方法促進了更優越的任務分解和自適應導航性能。我們在現實世界的背景中驗證了我們的框架，展示了其即使在有限的訓練數據下仍能保持高可靠性和語義意識的能力。
2605.28607.author: Susanna Cifani et.al.
2605.28607.authors: Susanna Cifani, Mario Luca Bernardi, Marta Cimitile
